brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid causal reasoning; Multi-level analysis covering collider bias, d-separation, Berkson's paradox, and intuitive explanation; Some self-monitoring via the injected reasoning context suppressing correlation-to-causation leaps; Verification through intuitive sanity check of what happens when conditioning on admitted students; Acknowledges the true relationship is none but doesn't explicitly quantify uncertainty; Systematically evaluates both methods and explains why Method 1 fails with specific causal graph reasoning; Complete derivation from causal graph structure through d-separation to conclusion, fully reproducible
